pi_items.source: Web search confirmed NGC 5195 is the dwarf companion galaxy to M51 (Whirlpool Galaxy)
Downloaded and verified the image file exists at wsr.imagej.net/images/m51.tif with Last-Modified: Jan 14, 2017
Analyzed actual m51.tif file using PIL Image library

Image analysis shows companion galaxy area ranges 531-4271 pixels depending on threshold choice. At typical threshold (mean + 0.5*std), companion area = 1501 pixels, which is > 1000. Range is 1000-8000 pixels.
Image analysis shows companion area never exceeds 4271 pixels across all tested thresholds, well below 8000 pixel maximum.
Image analysis shows companion galaxy Y-centroid positions: 199.1 (at mean+0.5*std), 179.2 (at mean+0.3*std), 180.6 (at mean+0.7*std). All values < 200.

pi_items.verified_value: 7381-17915 pixels depending on threshold choice; typically ~10000-15000
pi_items.source: Image analysis combined M51 + NGC 5195 areas: at mean+0.3*std = 17915, at mean+0.5*std = 10185, at mean+0.7*std = 7381 pixels

pi_items.verified_value: NGC 5195 is at top portion of image with Y-centroid 179-199, Y-range approximately 120-263
pi_items.source: Direct image analysis using scipy.ndimage segmentation and centroid calculation

pi_items.verified_value: M51 main spiral has Y-centroid 252-255 (roughly centered vertically in 510-pixel height)
pi_items.source: Image segmentation shows M51 Y-range approximately 193-314, centered around 254

privileged_info_summary: The m51.tif image is a real ImageJ sample image (320x510 pixels, 16-bit) containing the M51 Whirlpool Galaxy and its companion NGC 5195. NGC 5195 is positioned in the upper portion of the image with Y-centroid around 179-199 pixels. The companion galaxy has an area between 1500-4300 pixels depending on threshold selection, validly within the stated 1000-8000 pixel range. The two galaxies can be separated by thresholding, and the companion should be measured separately from the main spiral galaxy M51. A discrepancy exists: the metadata claims combined area is '~20000+' pixels, but actual measurements show 7000-18000 pixels depending on threshold, with typical values around 10000-15000 pixels. The verifier.py comment stating the image is '~510x510' is also incorrect; the actual dimensions are 320x510.
